Verified Facts from Sources
From References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt:

Butch provided pedigree timeline attachments.
Timeline bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records with numbered references to Butch’s copies.
Blue dots indicate census records.
Not all ancestors are shown; only early generations that fit page space.
Butch can provide a PDF catalog of record types.

Known Limitations

The .cdr files are not directly transcribed in this environment.
Person-level extraction from those files is pending export to PDF/image.
Current person-level data is intentionally minimal to avoid unsourced claims.

Session Addendum — 2026-04-22 Shared Intake Crawl
Scope
Performed external source reconnaissance against G:\My Drive\Thorpe Shared Intake (read-only crawl) to identify ingest-ready evidence and extraction priorities.
What Changed in This Session

No new genealogical claims were committed to People/ pages in this session.
Repository tracking artifacts were updated:

CHANGELOG.md updated with intake-crawl session note.
This handoff file updated with intake findings and action queue.



Files Reviewed (External Intake)

Pedigree Timeline/Misc Pedigree Timeline Files/SPICLINE.txt
Parsed content supplied during session for 2025 pedigree timeline pages and CensusSummaryIndividual pages.

Verified Items from Reviewed Sources

Parish register extracts include directly citable entries for Kelly/Kirby/Emblow lines with dates, places, and repository links.
Death index note confirms an indexed death record entry for William Henry Palmer (Waterloo, Iowa, 8 May 1927).
Census citation notes include UK and US references (HO107/RG9/RG10/RG13 and US roll/ED identifiers) tied to named individuals.
SPICLINE.txt provides a compact generation chain from Peter Spicer through Karen Lu Spicer and Robert AJ Thorpe.
Parsed timeline pages provide person name/date spans and marker categories that align with Butch’s earlier legend (certificate/census/obituary/burial indicators).

Uncertain or Pending Verification

OCR-derived or parsed timeline text may contain transcription and spelling noise; each claim still requires image/PDF cross-check.
Some entries are explicitly approximate (c., ?, before/after) and must remain marked as uncertain in downstream pages.
Living or recently deceased-person details (for example, modern obituaries) require conservative handling and user confirmation before broad publication.

Priority Next Steps After Crawl

Stage intake files into References/raw/inbox/ in batches by source type (Pedigree Timeline, Census, Certificates, Obituaries, BurialSites).
Start text-first extraction from .txt files and parsed timeline outputs into source notes under References/.
Create/expand People/ pages only with source-cited, confidence-labeled facts.
Keep People Directory and Search Index synchronized as new person/topic/reference pages are added.
Add follow-on handoff file when person-level ingestion begins so phase boundaries remain clear.

Home.title: Home
Home.content:
Thorpe Family Research Vault
Welcome to the Thorpe family research vault. This project is separate from the Copley family vault and is focused on building a source-grounded Thorpe genealogy.
Scope

Build Thorpe family person profiles from primary or near-primary records
Track source provenance and confidence levels
Preserve direct source files and extraction notes
Cross-link to Copley research only where family lines intersect

Initial Sources

People/Amy-E-Critton.title: Amy E Critton
People/Amy-E-Critton.content:
Amy E Critton
Biographical Profile

Name: Amy E Critton
Role in this project: Miller-line ancestor represented in repeated Walnut Township, Ohio census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Amy E. Critton as born 12 Jan 1801 and died 31 May 1874.
The 1850 Walnut Township, Fairfield County, Ohio table lists Anna MILLER (Amy) with Mathias Miller and children.
The 1860 and 1870 Walnut Township entries continue Amy/Ama Miller in the Mathias Miller household sequence.
The 1850 extract cites Series M432, Roll 677, Page 197.

Research Gaps

Confirm Critton-to-Miller identity linkage and name-form consistency (Amy/Anna/Ama).
Validate each decade’s household continuity from image-level census pages.
Reconcile occupation and property fields where OCR appears uncertain.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p21-p30
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/Andrew-Ault.title: Andrew Ault
People/Andrew-Ault.content:
Andrew Ault
Biographical Profile

Name: Andrew Ault
Role in this project: Direct ancestor listed in the census-summary index and early US census table extracts.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Andrew Ault as born 2 Apr 1769 and died 28 Mar 1852.
The 1850 census extract (Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio) lists Andrew Ault as male, age 82, occupation farmer, born New York.
The same 1850 household includes Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio).
The extract cites Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether the listed 1850 household member Amy Palmer is a granddaughter, niece, or other relationship.
Verify the 1769 birth and 1852 death values against primary vital/church/cemetery records.
Add residence chronology from additional census years where available.

Sources

Role in this project: Direct ancestor with multi-decade US census entries in the census summary.

A census-summary entry gives Elizabeth Plomey Ault as born 31 Oct 1841 and died 5 Mar 1926.
The 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio extract includes Elizabeth Ault age 8 in the Frederick and Mary Ault household.
The 1860 Salisbury Township extract includes Eliza Ault age 18 in a household with Mary Ault and Gilmore family members.
The 1880 Afton Township, Cherokee County, Iowa extract lists Elizabeth Tallman as wife of Miller M. Tallman, with daughter Lena Tallman in the household.
The 1900 Sioux City, Woodbury County, Iowa extract lists Elizabeth P. Tallman with birth month/year Oct 1841.
The 1910 and 1920 Iowa Soldiers’ Home extracts in Marshall County list Elizabeth P. Tallman with Miller M. Tallman.

Research Gaps

References/Butch-Thorpe-Email.content:
Butch Thorpe Email
Source file: References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt
Sender and Context

Sender signs as Butch (nickname for Robert “Butch” Thorpe).
Message sent to Zach with interpretation guidance for attached pedigree timeline files.

Extracted Statements

Attachments are intended as a resource-finding aid and include pedigrees.
Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Not all ancestors with records are shown; only those from earliest generations that fit on the page.
Red dots denote official record types, with nearby numbers referencing Butch’s personal copies.
Blue dots denote census records at timeline dates.
Butch can provide a PDF describing the record types he has collected.

Notes
This email provides interpretation metadata for the .cdr pedigree files and should be cited when describing how timeline markers are encoded.

Context

Files were staged from shared intake in text-note form.
These notes compile citation references for UK and selected US census appearances tied to Thorpe-line ancestors.

Extracted Statements

The notes include UK citation references for 1841, 1861, 1871, and 1901 census years using Class/Piece/Folio/Page formats (for example HO107, RG9, RG10, RG13).
Individuals listed include Bellamy, Sorrell, Thorogood, Emblow, Kelly, Whitfield, and Munson/Monson variants.
The notes include US citation entries for 1900 with roll/page/enumeration district formats and names including Clyde Thorpe and Joseph Prior.
English Census Notes.txt links individual image filenames to citation metadata, indicating potential image-level validation paths.

Uncertainty Notes

Person-name spelling varies across files, including Monson/Munson and Witfield/Whitfield.
Notes include possible or inferred matches in some filename annotations and should be treated as provisional until image checks are complete.

Processing scope

Parsed and reviewed pages 1-10 of CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf.
This is a partial extraction pass focused on high-confidence entries in early pages.

Extracted statements

The document title page identifies the work as a census summary for individual ancestors of Lancelot and Wendolyn Thorpe, compiled by Robert Thorpe in October 2009.
The introduction states the intended census coverage range is 1841 through 1930 and that the index lists direct ancestors.
The contents pages include direct-ancestor entries for AULT, Andrew, AULT, Elizabeth Plomey, and AULT, Frederick.
Page 9 includes an 1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio household for Andrew Ault (age 82, farmer, born New York) with Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio), cited to Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.
Page 10 includes an Elizabeth Plomey Ault section with census entries for 1850, 1860, 1880, 1900, 1910, and 1920, including repeated Tallman-household entries for Miller M. Tallman and Elizabeth P. Tallman.
Page 10 entries identify Lena/Lena H. Tallman as daughter in 1880 and 1900 records and include birthplace fields and household relationships.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing may contain minor transcription artifacts in abbreviations and column headers.
Extracted statements are limited to pages 1-10 and do not yet include deeper person-specific pages after page 10.
Compiler contact details visible in the introduction were not propagated to person pages.

This extraction pass covers entries from Ault, Bangle, Baxter/Rowland, Bellamy, Beneworth/Thorogood, and Blake/Lemmon sections.

Extracted statements

Page 11 includes AULT, Frederick with the 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio household and citation Series M432, Roll 710, Page 175.
Pages 12-13 include BANGLE, Elizabeth Maria and BANGLE, Louis S Jr with recurring 1860 Michigan and 1870/1880 Wisconsin household entries and citations including M653 Roll 1407 and M593 Roll 1712.
Page 14 includes BAXTER, Matilda with 1850 Indiana and 1860/1870 Iowa Rowland household entries and citation Series M432, Roll 154, Page 241.
Pages 15-18 include Bellamy branch entries (Henry James, James, James Archibald, Richard) with repeated 19th/early 20th century Lincolnshire/Peterborough census extracts and multiple UK source references (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13, RG14 tokens as listed).
Page 19 includes BENEWORTH, Hannah with 1841/1861 Thorogood-linked entries in Felsted, Essex.
Page 20 includes BLAKE, Rebecca with an 1850 Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio Lemmon household entry and citation Series M432, Roll 726, Page 476.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing introduces visible spelling and formatting inconsistencies in some names and place strings.
Several UK entries on these pages contain incomplete piece/folio/page fields in the compiled table text and require image-level verification.
Some household role interpretations are compiler-normalized and should be treated as provisional until direct page-image checks are complete.

This extraction pass covers Brooks/Ault crossover, Burdick-Risden, Burgett-Spicer, Bangle/Caswell, Critton-Miller, Emblow/Kelly, Filkins/Johnson-Spicer, and Greenwood mortality entries.

Extracted statements

Page 21 (BROOKS, Elizabeth) reuses the 1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio Ault household extract with Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.
Page 22 (BURDICK, Saphronia) includes a 1870 Lapeer, Michigan 4th Ward RISDEN household entry with Watson, Nancy, Saphrona, and John E. Risden.
Pages 23-24 (BURGETT, Mary and BURGETT, William) include 1850 Iowa County, Iowa Brush Run Township Bergett household entries; page 23 also includes Spicer household tables for 1860/1870/1880/1900/1910 linked to Mary Burgett in mother/mother-in-law roles.
Page 25 (CASWELL, Angeline) includes repeated Bangle household entries (1860/1870/1880) plus a 1900 Eau Claire, Wisconsin entry for Cazwell BANGLE.
Page 26 (CRITTON, Amy E.) includes 1850/1860/1870 Walnut Township, Fairfield County, Ohio Miller-household entries with Mathias and Amy/Anna Miller.
Pages 27-28 include EMBLOW, Joseph and EMBLOW, Martha with UK census entries from 1841 through 1901 and citations including HO107, RG9, and RG10 references in listed years.
Page 29 (FILKINS, Catherine) includes 1850 New York Nunda Johnson household and 1860 West Sparta entry containing Catherine Johnson.
Page 30 (GREENWOOD, Mary) includes an 1850 Iowa mortality schedule entry for Mary Bergett with death month/cause notes.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing introduces visible spelling and formatting inconsistencies in names and occupations.
Some UK entries in this slice include incomplete piece/folio/page fields and require image-level verification.
Several entries appear compiler-crosslinked across surnames and need identity continuity checks before deeper relationship assertions.

Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
This page tracks the pedigree timeline files provided by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and documents what they contain.
Files Provided

References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr
References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr

Butch’s Notes on How to Read the Timelines
Per Butch’s email:

Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records.
Numbers by red dots refer to Butch’s personal record copies.
Blue dots indicate census records corresponding to the date marked by vertical lines.
The charts show selected early generations that fit on each page, not all ancestors with records.

Current Limitation
The files are CorelDRAW (.cdr) documents and require export to PDF or image before structured transcription into person pages can proceed.
Requested Follow-up Sources

PDF list of record types collected by Butch
Mapping/key for numbered red-dot record references
